claims.medication: Tecfidera 240mg twice daily, Amitriptyline 25mg at night, Modafinil 200mg morning

notes.author: j.patel
notes.content: New PIP claim received. Claimant diagnosed with RRMS in 2022. Reports significant fatigue affecting daily activities. Currently working part-time (15 hrs/week) but struggling. PIP2 form describes difficulty with food preparation, bathing, and mobility on bad days.

notes.author: j.patel
notes.content: GP letter received from Dr Kaur at Riverside Surgery. Confirms MS diagnosis, fatigue severity rated 7/10 most days. States patient has unpredictable relapses lasting 2-6 weeks where mobility is severely affected. Recommends enhanced mobility component.

notes.author: j.patel
notes.content: Neurologist report from Dr Shah at Royal Manchester. Details cognitive fatigue, bilateral leg weakness during relapses, and bladder urgency. MRI shows active lesions. Report supports substantial daily living needs.

notes.author: m.khan
notes.content: Case review: strong medical evidence. Fluctuating condition makes scoring complex — need to assess on worst days per PIP guidance. Approaching 75-day SLA. Prioritise for assessment this week.

evidence.description: Dr Kaur, Riverside Surgery — confirms MS, fatigue 7/10
Dr Shah, Neurology, Royal Manchester Infirmary

claims.medication: Sertraline 150mg daily, Propranolol 40mg as needed, Zopiclone 7.5mg at night

notes.author: r.singh
notes.content: New claim. Claimant is a former security officer who developed PTSD and severe anxiety following a workplace assault in 2023. Cannot leave home unaccompanied. Reports panic attacks 3-4 times per week. PIP2 describes inability to plan journeys, engage with others, or manage finances independently.

notes.author: r.singh
notes.content: Community Mental Health Team letter received. Confirms severe agoraphobia — claimant has not left home alone since August 2024. Attending CBT via video calls. Team assesses him as having substantial care needs.

evidence.description: Completed with help from support worker

claims.medication: Metformin 1000mg twice daily, Naproxen 500mg twice daily, Amlodipine 10mg, Co-codamol as needed

notes.author: j.patel
notes.content: Reassessment — existing award expiring April 2025. Currently on standard daily living + standard mobility. Condition has worsened per claimant. Now uses walking frame indoors and wheelchair for distances over 20 metres.

notes.author: j.patel
notes.content: Orthopaedic report confirms bilateral knee replacement deferred due to diabetes management. Mobility significantly reduced since last assessment. GP confirms pain level 8/10 on most days.

notes.author: j.patel
notes.content: All evidence received. Straightforward reassessment — condition clearly deteriorated. Recommending upgrade to enhanced mobility based on 20m walking distance with aids.

evidence.description: Completed by claimant with daughter's help
Dr Williams — pain management and diabetes

notes.author: r.singh
notes.content: New claim from young adult. Diagnosed age 16, recently reassessed as Level 2 ASC. Lives with parents. Struggles significantly with social communication, managing finances, and planning journeys. Mother reports he cannot safely cook, frequently forgets medication, and becomes extremely distressed in unfamiliar environments.

evidence.description: Completed by mother as appointed person
Diagnostic report from Dr Lim, Adult Autism Service — OUTSTANDING

claims.medication: Pregabalin 300mg twice daily, Duloxetine 60mg, Tramadol 50mg as needed, Omeprazole 20mg

notes.author: j.patel
notes.content: Mandatory reconsideration requested. Original decision: 4 points daily living (standard not met), 0 mobility. Claimant disputes — says assessor did not account for fluctuating nature of fibromyalgia and that face-to-face assessment was on a 'good day'. Original report noted claimant 'appeared comfortable' but claimant states she was masking severe pain.

notes.author: j.patel
notes.content: New evidence submitted: pain diary covering 3 months, rheumatology letter stating flare-ups occur 15-20 days per month with severe fatigue and pain. Letter explicitly states claimant's function on assessment day was not representative.

notes.author: m.khan
notes.content: MR review: original assessment appears to have underscored. New rheumatology evidence is compelling. Fibromyalgia fluctuation not adequately captured. Recommend full rescoring with 'bad day' descriptors. This is a common appeal pattern — 65% of PIP tribunals overturn the DWP decision.

evidence.description: Dr Chen, Rheumatology — new evidence for MR
Dr Patel, confirming medication and impact

notes.author: system
notes.content: Claim submitted via online portal. Documents uploaded: PIP2 form, GP letter, MRI report scan. Awaiting caseworker assignment.

evidence.description: Dr Jones, confirming disc herniation and pain management
